summary: Hero Orchestration Refactor - All tests passed. The new orchestrated layout shows ONE Primary Hero Card (featured action) + ONE 'Also on your radar' strip (supporting items). Previously multiple hero cards stacked vertically, now computeHeroSelection picks the highest-priority hero and suppresses rest to radar strip.
critical_code_review_comments: heroOrchestrator.js: computeHeroSelection correctly implements priority order (committed=1 > coach_task=2 > coach_flag=3 > hot_opportunity=4 > ...)
heroOrchestrator.js: Empty state fallback returns 'Start outreach to this program' when no hero candidates exist
PrimaryHeroCard.js: Correctly handles both committed (centered, gold accent) and non-committed (icon + text layout) rendering
RadarStrip.js: Renders suppressed items as compact strip with dot indicators and tag labels
JourneyPage.js: Line 502-503 correctly renders PrimaryHeroCard + RadarStrip instead of old stacked cards

success_rate.frontend: 100% (4 schools tested - Stanford, Creighton, Florida, UCLA)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Hero orchestration refactor complete. Key changes: (1) Only ONE primary-hero-card per page, (2) Suppressed items appear in radar-strip, (3) Empty state shows 'Start outreach to this program', (4) Getting Started Checklist still appears for new schools, (5) Coach Watch card in sidebar unchanged.
